title: rubus hispidulus
intro: Rubus hispidulus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a species of bramble in the genus Rubus. It is a deciduous shrub native to North America, with edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Rubus hispidulus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
